cards.headline: US added just 57,000 jobs in June
cards.summary: US employers added only 57,000 jobs in June, well below expectations and slower than the previous two months, according to the Labor Department. The unemployment rate ticked down slightly to 4.2%. The figures signal a cooling labor market amid broader economic uncertainty.

quiz.prompt: How many jobs did US employers add in June, according to the Labor Department?
quiz.explanation: The Labor Department reported that employers added 57,000 jobs in June, lower than expected and slower than prior months.

cards.headline: Trump earned $1 billion from crypto; investors lost
cards.summary: President Trump earned more than $1 billion in 2025 from cryptocurrency ventures, according to NPR reporting. However, thousands of investors who bought into those same cryptocurrencies stand to lose big. The contrast has drawn significant attention to the personal financial gains made by the president through digital assets.

quiz.prompt: How much did President Trump earn from cryptocurrency ventures in 2025?
quiz.explanation: NPR reported that President Trump earned more than $1 billion in 2025 from cryptocurrency ventures, while many of his investors stand to lose big.

cards.headline: EasyJet agrees in principle to £5.2–5.5bn takeover
cards.summary: EasyJet has agreed in principle to a takeover deal by US investment firm Castlelake, valued at between £5.2 billion and £5.5 billion. The airline had previously rejected four takeover offers from Castlelake. The deal would take the UK's biggest low-cost carrier private.

quiz.prompt: How many takeover offers had EasyJet previously rejected from Castlelake before agreeing in principle?
quiz.explanation: EasyJet had previously rejected four takeover offers from Castlelake before agreeing in principle to the deal.

cards.headline: Samsung profits surge 1,800% but shares fall
cards.summary: Samsung reported a roughly 1,800-fold — or 19-fold depending on the metric — jump in profits as AI chip sales soared, but its shares fell sharply as some investors had expected an even stronger performance. The disappointing market reaction triggered a broader sell-off in AI chip stocks. Futures for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq also slipped on the news.

quiz.prompt: Why did Samsung's shares fall despite a massive profit jump?
options: The company issued a profit warning
Some investors had expected an even stronger performance
quiz.explanation: Samsung's shares fell because some investors had expected an even stronger performance, despite the dramatic surge in profits driven by AI chip sales.

cards.headline: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs in major restructure
cards.summary: Microsoft announced it is cutting 4,800 jobs — equivalent to 2.1% of its global workforce — in what it described as a significant restructure. The layoffs include 1,600 immediate job losses at the Xbox division, which is also being shrunk. The move signals a major strategic shift at one of the world's largest technology companies.

quiz.prompt: What percentage of Microsoft's workforce do the 4,800 job cuts represent?
quiz.explanation: Microsoft's 4,800 job cuts equate to 2.1% of its global workforce, according to the BBC.

cards.headline: Trump refuses to renew North American trade pact
cards.summary: President Trump has refused to renew the USMCA trade pact — the North American free trade agreement he once championed — opting instead to keep it alive on short leashes of annual reviews rather than a longer-term renewal. The decision adds significant uncertainty for the US auto industry, where rules of origin determining tariff eligibility are a major concern. Ford's CEO has called for a level playing field with Toyota and GM imports as the trade talks reopen.

quiz.prompt: What did Trump opt for instead of renewing the USMCA long-term?
quiz.explanation: Trump opted to keep USMCA alive on short-leash annual reviews rather than agreeing to a longer-term renewal.

cards.headline: Half of Americans struggle to afford groceries
cards.summary: About half of Americans are struggling to afford groceries and gas, according to an exclusive poll conducted by Harris for the Guardian. Around 57% of those surveyed also believe the economy is worsening, painting a grim picture of the cost-of-living crisis. The findings suggest the affordability crunch is cutting across broad segments of the population.

quiz.prompt: According to the Harris poll for the Guardian, what share of Americans believe the economy is worsening?
quiz.explanation: The poll found that about 57% of Americans believe the economy is worsening, alongside widespread struggles to afford groceries and gas.

cards.headline: Tesla Q2 deliveries beat Wall Street expectations
cards.summary: Tesla posted stronger-than-expected second-quarter delivery figures, surpassing Wall Street estimates and suggesting its auto business is regaining momentum. The results come after two consecutive years of annual sales declines and amid a period of significant public backlash against CEO Elon Musk. The strong figures indicate the Musk backlash may be cooling among car buyers.

quiz.prompt: What do Tesla's strong Q2 delivery figures follow?
options: Two straight years of annual sales declines
quiz.explanation: Tesla's better-than-expected Q2 deliveries come after two consecutive years of annual sales declines, suggesting its auto business is recovering.

cards.headline: SpaceX valuation gap exceeds $1 trillion between underwriters
cards.summary: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, the two lead underwriters on SpaceX's initial public offering, have a valuation gap of more than $1 trillion between them as both initiated coverage with buy ratings. SpaceX also joined the Nasdaq-100, meaning it could start appearing in investors' 401(k) retirement accounts. The index addition adds a highly volatile private-turned-public company to widely held portfolios.

quiz.prompt: What is the valuation gap between SpaceX's two lead IPO underwriters, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley?
quiz.explanation: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley have a valuation gap of more than $1 trillion on SpaceX as both initiated coverage with buy ratings.

cards.headline: Egg producers settle DOJ price-fixing case
cards.summary: Seventeen US states and the Department of Justice have settled a price-fixing case against major egg producers. Egg prices soared significantly in recent years before falling sharply. The settlement closes a high-profile antitrust action that drew widespread attention as consumers faced dramatically higher costs at grocery stores.

quiz.prompt: How many US states joined the Department of Justice in settling the egg price-fixing case?
quiz.explanation: Seventeen states joined the Department of Justice in settling the price-fixing complaint against major egg producers.

cards.headline: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs, shrinks Xbox division
cards.summary: Microsoft is eliminating approximately 4,800 jobs, roughly 2.1% of its global workforce, in what it calls a significant restructure. The cuts heavily affect Xbox, with around 3,200 gaming employees let go — about 20% of the gaming division — as the company refocuses on its biggest franchises. The layoffs are the latest in a series that has stoked broader fears about AI replacing workers.

quiz.prompt: What percentage of Microsoft's global workforce do the 4,800 layoffs represent?
quiz.explanation: The snippets consistently state the cuts equate to roughly 2.1% of Microsoft's global workforce.

cards.headline: Watchdogs warn parents over AI child abuse imagery
cards.summary: The UK's National Crime Agency and a safety watchdog have issued guidance warning parents against publicly sharing images of their children online, citing a growing threat of those images being used to create AI-generated child sexual abuse material. Sophisticated nudification apps are enabling online predators to turn ordinary photos into explicit content. The guidance comes amid a documented rise in such material appearing online.

quiz.prompt: Which UK agency issued the guidance warning parents about sharing children's images online?
quiz.explanation: The snippets state the NCA (National Crime Agency) issued the guidance alongside a safety watchdog.

cards.headline: AI tools found to alter meaning of users' drafts
cards.summary: A study has found that AI writing tools are subtly changing the meaning of users' messages on sensitive political topics including abortion and climate change. Researchers warn that even small AI-driven alterations in drafting could spread rapidly across platforms and create long-term shifts in public opinion. The findings raise fresh concerns about the influence AI tools have over political discourse.

quiz.prompt: What concern did researchers raise about AI altering users' draft messages?
options: Small changes could spread widely and shift public opinion
quiz.explanation: The study found that small AI-driven changes in drafting could spread rapidly and create long-term shifts in public opinion.

cards.headline: Australia's child social media ban barely working, study finds
cards.summary: A new study found that six months after Australia introduced a world-first ban on children using social media, the policy has barely affected youth access to platforms including Facebook, Instagram and YouTube. In response, Australia is now planning to double the potential fines for platforms that fail to prevent children from holding accounts. The government is also moving to strengthen the laws underpinning the ban.

quiz.prompt: What is Australia doing in response to evidence its child social media ban is failing?
options: Doubling potential fines for non-compliant platforms
quiz.explanation: Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms that fail to prevent children from holding accounts.

cards.headline: US partly lifts export ban on Anthropic's top AI model
cards.summary: The Trump administration has partially lifted an export ban on Anthropic's most advanced AI model, asserting a new level of government influence over which companies can access cutting-edge AI. OpenAI has also agreed to let the administration screen users of its newest model. The moves signal a broader US government effort to control the global distribution of frontier AI systems.

quiz.prompt: What did OpenAI agree to allow the Trump administration to do regarding its new model?
quiz.explanation: According to the snippet, OpenAI agreed to let the administration screen users of its new model.

cards.headline: Google ordered to pay €4.1bn Android antitrust fine
cards.summary: Google has been ordered to pay a €4.1 billion fine after a court ruled it used Android to block rivals. A Google spokesperson said the judgement fails to recognise the firm's significant investment to ensure Android remains open. The ruling is one of the largest antitrust penalties handed down against a major tech company in Europe.

quiz.prompt: How much was Google ordered to pay in the Android antitrust ruling?
quiz.explanation: The snippet states Google must pay a €4.1bn fine for using Android to block rivals.

cards.headline: WhatsApp to allow chatting via usernames, not numbers
cards.summary: WhatsApp is rolling out a new usernames feature globally over the coming months, allowing users to connect with each other without sharing their phone numbers. Users can already begin reserving their preferred usernames ahead of the full rollout. The change is a significant privacy shift for a platform that has historically required phone number exchange to initiate contact.

quiz.prompt: What does WhatsApp's new username feature allow users to avoid sharing?
quiz.explanation: The snippets state the feature will let people chat by swapping usernames instead of phone numbers.

cards.headline: Memory chip demand signals no end to AI boom
cards.summary: Earnings from Micron, the largest US memory chip maker, signal no end in sight to demand for the microchips at the heart of the AI investment boom, despite market fears of an AI bubble. Meanwhile, South Korean rival SK Hynix is also riding the AI surge to a multibillion-dollar US IPO. The results suggest the infrastructure underpinning AI continues to expand rapidly.

quiz.prompt: Which US company's earnings were cited as evidence of continued strong AI chip demand?
quiz.explanation: The snippet states earnings from Micron, the biggest US memory chip maker, signal no end in sight to demand for AI chips.

cards.headline: First AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
cards.summary: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack in what is described as the first known instance of its kind. However, new details reveal a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied the AI agent with its instructions. The case highlights that while AI is lowering the technical barrier for cybercrime, human direction remains central.

quiz.prompt: In the first reported AI-executed ransomware attack, what role did a human still play?
options: Nothing — the AI was fully autonomous
The human chose the victim and set up the infrastructure
The human only wrote the ransom note
quiz.explanation: The snippet states a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied the AI with its instructions.

cards.headline: Supreme Court lets Texas restrict minors' app downloads
cards.summary: The US Supreme Court has allowed Texas's App Store Accountability Act to take effect while legal challenges continue in lower courts. The law requires minors to obtain parental permission before downloading most apps. The ruling marks a significant moment in the ongoing national debate over children's access to digital platforms.

quiz.prompt: What does Texas's App Store Accountability Act require minors to do before downloading most apps?
quiz.explanation: The snippet states the law requires minors to have their parents' permission to download most apps.

cards.headline: Trump pressures NATO allies as Ankara summit begins
cards.summary: President Trump arrived in Ankara, Turkey, for the NATO summit, where his dissatisfaction with the alliance set a tense tone. Europe is increasingly being forced to take primary responsibility for its own defense as the illusion of appeasing Trump has faded. Ukraine's defense needs and member spending commitments are expected to dominate the agenda.

quiz.prompt: Where is the NATO summit being held?
quiz.explanation: Multiple sources confirm the NATO summit is taking place in Ankara, Turkey.

cards.headline: Russia pounds Kyiv; Ukraine seeks NATO air defenses
cards.summary: Russian strikes killed more than 20 people in Kyiv and the surrounding area, leaving a gaping hole in at least one residential building. President Zelensky is using the NATO summit in Turkey to press allies for more interceptor missiles and anti-ballistic air defense systems. The escalation comes as Ukraine war diplomacy returns to center stage.

quiz.prompt: What specific military equipment is Ukraine pressing NATO allies to provide after the latest Russian strikes?
options: Interceptor missiles and anti-ballistic air defense systems
quiz.explanation: Zelensky is pressing NATO for more interceptor missiles and anti-ballistic air defense systems following the deadly Russian strikes on Kyiv.

cards.headline: China's South Pacific missile test alarms Australia, Pacific
cards.summary: China test-launched a long-range ballistic missile in the South Pacific using a dummy warhead, raising concerns across the region. Australian Prime Minister Albanese condemned the test as destabilising, saying the missile could have caused considerable damage if weaponised. The launch occurred the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty widely seen as a counter to Chinese influence in the Pacific.

quiz.prompt: What event happened on the same day as China's South Pacific missile test?
options: Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty
The US imposed new sanctions on China
quiz.explanation: The missile test took place the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty aimed at countering Chinese influence in the Pacific.

cards.headline: Explosions wound 18 in Damascus during Macron's Syria visit
cards.summary: At least 18 people, including four police officers, were wounded in explosions in Damascus while French President Macron was visiting Syria. The blasts occurred near the hotel Macron had reportedly been staying in, shortly after he departed for the presidential palace. Macron's office said he was safe and that his meeting with Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa would continue as scheduled.

quiz.prompt: How many people were wounded in the Damascus explosions during Macron's visit?
quiz.explanation: Syria's Interior Ministry confirmed at least 18 people, including four police officers, were wounded in the blasts.

cards.headline: Iran stages massive political spectacle for Khamenei's funeral
cards.summary: Three days of public mourning and huge funeral processions took place in Tehran following the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Analysts described the event as a political spectacle Iran's leaders deliberately orchestrated for the world to see, conveying themes of resistance and revenge. Large crowds lined the streets of Tehran for the procession.

quiz.prompt: How many days of public mourning did Iran hold for Ayatollah Khamenei?
quiz.explanation: The BBC reported three days of public mourning in Tehran following the death of Iran's Supreme Leader.

cards.headline: Trump confirms he asked FIFA to review Balogun's ban
cards.summary: President Trump confirmed he contacted FIFA head Gianni Infantino to discuss the suspension of US striker Folarin Balogun, who had been banned for one match. FIFA subsequently allowed Balogun to play against Belgium, drawing sharp criticism from European politicians and soccer officials who accused Trump of corrupt interference. Infantino acknowledged the conversation but insisted the reinstatement was not the result of White House pressure.

quiz.prompt: What did FIFA ultimately decide regarding Balogun's one-match suspension?
options: Referred it to an independent panel
quiz.explanation: FIFA allowed Balogun to play against Belgium after Trump confirmed he had asked FIFA to review the suspension, sparking accusations of political interference.

cards.headline: French court opens door for Le Pen presidential run
cards.summary: A Paris appeals court found Marine Le Pen guilty of misusing public funds but reduced the length of her ban from holding public office. The ruling opens a potential path for her to run for the French presidency, though the court also ordered her to wear an electronic ankle tag—a condition Le Pen has previously said would rule her out from running. The decision leaves her political future in an uncertain position.

quiz.prompt: What condition did the Paris court attach to its revised ruling on Le Pen?
quiz.explanation: The appeals court reduced Le Pen's ineligibility period but ordered her to wear an electronic ankle tag, which she has previously said would prevent her from running.

cards.headline: Tanker attacked in Strait of Hormuz amid diplomatic tensions
cards.summary: A tanker was set ablaze after being struck by a projectile in the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian state television said the vessel came under attack after ignoring warnings but did not directly claim responsibility for the assault. The incident raises fresh concerns about marine traffic and the security of one of the world's most critical shipping lanes.

quiz.prompt: Who did Iranian state television suggest was responsible for the tanker attack in the Strait of Hormuz?
options: Iran did not directly claim responsibility
quiz.explanation: Iranian state television said the tanker was attacked after ignoring warnings but did not directly claim responsibility for the assault.

cards.headline: Democratic Senate candidate faces assault allegation, party exodus
cards.summary: Maine Democratic Senate nominee Graham Platner is facing mounting pressure to withdraw from the race after Politico reported a woman's allegation that he sexually assaulted her in 2021, which he has denied as categorically untrue. Maine's Democratic Party leadership called on him to drop out, and prominent Democratic lawmakers including Rep. Ro Khanna withdrew their endorsements. Platner said he was taking time to reflect on his candidacy.

quiz.prompt: What has Graham Platner said in response to the sexual assault allegation?
options: He denied the claim as categorically untrue
quiz.explanation: Platner denied the allegations as categorically untrue while saying he was taking time to reflect on his candidacy.

cards.headline: Sri Lanka prison riots kill 26, injure over 100
cards.summary: Two days of violence at Negombo Prison in Sri Lanka left 26 people dead and more than 100 injured. The riots are described as the worst prison violence in the country in years. No further details on the cause of the unrest were provided in available reports.

quiz.prompt: At which prison did the deadly riots in Sri Lanka occur?
quiz.explanation: The BBC reported that the deadly two-day riots took place at Negombo Prison, killing 26 and injuring more than 100.

cards.headline: Belgium thrash USA 4-1 amid Balogun furore
cards.summary: Belgium eliminated co-hosts USA from the World Cup with a 4-1 victory, a result overshadowed by controversy over FIFA's decision to lift Folarin Balogun's red-card suspension. Belgium's Nicolas Raskin said the ruling fired his team up, and Belgian players appeared to mock both FIFA and Donald Trump after the win. US coach Mauricio Pochettino said he felt 'disappointed with too many people' who brought 'politics and manipulation' into the game.

quiz.prompt: What was the final score when Belgium eliminated the USA from the World Cup?
quiz.explanation: Belgium beat the USA 4-1 in the round of 16, with a goalkeeping error from Matt Freese gifting Belgium their third goal.

cards.headline: FIFA and UEFA clash over Balogun red-card reversal
cards.summary: FIFA hit back at UEFA in an escalating war of words over the decision to lift Folarin Balogun's suspension, arguing that overturning red cards is a common measure and that UEFA had not previously raised any 'red line' on the issue. La Liga president Javier Tebas also criticised the 'complicit silence' surrounding FIFA in the wake of the controversy. The saga dominated discussion around the USA's exit from their home World Cup.

quiz.prompt: Who criticised the 'complicit silence' surrounding FIFA after the Balogun controversy?
quiz.explanation: La Liga president Javier Tebas criticised the 'complicit silence' surrounding FIFA in the wake of the Balogun red-card reversal controversy.

cards.headline: Ronaldo confirms 2026 World Cup was his last
cards.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo confirmed he has played his last World Cup game after Portugal were knocked out 1-0 by Spain through a Mikel Merino stoppage-time winner. Ronaldo said he would not make any 'rash decisions' on his overall Portugal future, while coach Roberto Martínez also stepped down following the defeat. Spain advance to a quarter-final against Belgium.

quiz.prompt: Who scored the winning goal that eliminated Portugal and ended Ronaldo's World Cup career?
quiz.explanation: Mikel Merino scored a stoppage-time winner as a super-sub to send Spain through and end Portugal's World Cup.

cards.headline: Fery makes Wimbledon history as British wildcard
cards.summary: Arthur Fery became the first British wildcard to reach a Grand Slam quarter-final after beating Grigor Dimitrov in a five-set thriller at Wimbledon, winning 7-5, 3-6, 4-6, 6-4, 7-6. Fery came from a set down to complete the upset in nearly four hours, and will next face ninth seed Flavio Cobolli. His run has been described as a fairytale after British players had a difficult start to this year's tournament.

quiz.prompt: Who did Arthur Fery beat to reach the Wimbledon quarter-finals?
quiz.explanation: Fery beat Grigor Dimitrov in five sets to become the first British wildcard to reach a Grand Slam quarter-final.

cards.headline: 9.1 million watch England's win over Mexico
cards.summary: England beat Mexico to reach the World Cup quarter-finals, with a peak UK audience of 9.1 million watching overnight. Ten of England's 11 goals at the tournament have been scored by either Harry Kane or Jude Bellingham, highlighting the team's dependence on the duo. England now face Norway in the quarter-finals, with medics working to fast-track Reece James's recovery for the right-back position.

quiz.prompt: How many of England's 11 World Cup goals were scored by Kane or Bellingham combined?
quiz.explanation: According to the reports, 10 of England's 11 goals at the World Cup were scored by either Harry Kane or Jude Bellingham.

cards.headline: Messi and Salah meet in World Cup last 16
cards.summary: Lionel Messi's Argentina face Mohamed Salah's Egypt in a round-of-16 tie, billed as the first international meeting between the two modern legends. Egypt qualified after a historic victory over Australia, and Egyptian fans have adopted the rallying cry 'They have Messi, but we have Salah.' The match represents a potentially defining moment for both players on the world stage.

quiz.prompt: How did Egypt reach their round-of-16 tie against Argentina?
quiz.explanation: Egypt reached the round of 16 after a historic victory over Australia, setting up the Messi vs Salah clash.

cards.headline: Mbappé condemns Paraguayan senator's racist attack
cards.summary: France's Kylian Mbappé hit out at a Paraguayan senator who called him a 'colonised Cameroonian,' describing her as a 'despicable woman.' The French Football Federation announced it would file criminal charges in response to the attack. The incident drew widespread attention during the ongoing World Cup.

quiz.prompt: What action did the French Football Federation announce following the racist attack on Mbappé?
quiz.explanation: The French Football Federation announced it would file criminal charges in response to the Paraguayan senator's racist comments targeting Mbappé.

cards.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo joins Miami Heat from Bucks
cards.summary: Two-time NBA Most Valuable Player Giannis Antetokounmpo has signed with the Miami Heat after leaving the Milwaukee Bucks. The move is one of the biggest NBA transactions in recent memory, reshaping the league's power balance. No further contract details were provided in the reports.

quiz.prompt: How many times has Giannis Antetokounmpo won the NBA MVP award?
quiz.explanation: Giannis Antetokounmpo is described as a two-time NBA Most Valuable Player upon his move to the Miami Heat.

cards.headline: Safety car ending threatens F1 rules rethink
cards.summary: The British Grand Prix at Silverstone ended under the safety car, denying fans a potentially thrilling finale as Charles Leclerc took the win. The anticlimactic finish has raised questions about F1's current regulations, with commentators suggesting it could send the sport back to the drawing board on rules. McLaren's Lando Norris, who finished fourth, admitted the team had 'a hell of a lot to improve' after 'pretty shocking' pace.

quiz.prompt: Who won the British Grand Prix at Silverstone despite the safety car ending?
quiz.explanation: Charles Leclerc won the British Grand Prix, though the race ended under the safety car, denying spectators a competitive finish.

cards.headline: NRL signs record-breaking $5.3bn broadcast deal
cards.summary: The NRL has announced a landmark $5.3 billion broadcast rights deal with Nine Entertainment and Foxtel, described as a seven-year agreement worth around $750 million annually from 2028. NRL chief Peter V'landys called it 'a defining moment for rugby league.' The deal sets a new financial benchmark for Australian sport.

quiz.prompt: Approximately how much is the NRL's new broadcast deal worth annually?
quiz.explanation: The seven-year NRL broadcast deal with Nine and Foxtel is worth around $750 million annually from 2028.

cards.headline: Trump arrives at NATO summit amid alliance tensions
cards.summary: President Trump arrived in Ankara for the NATO summit, where defence spending and support for Ukraine are expected to top the agenda. Trump expressed dissatisfaction with NATO allies while also saying he did not want their help in the US war on Iran. Ukrainian President Zelensky plans to use the meeting to press allies for more air defence systems and interceptor missiles following intense Russian strikes.

quiz.prompt: What is Zelensky's primary goal at the NATO summit in Turkey?
options: Pressing for more air defence systems and interceptor missiles
Requesting ground troops from member states
quiz.explanation: According to the snippets, Zelensky plans to use the NATO meeting to argue he needs more interceptor missiles and air defence systems after intense Russian strikes.

cards.headline: Explosions wound 18 in Damascus during Macron visit
cards.summary: A series of explosions rocked Syria's capital Damascus while French President Emmanuel Macron was visiting, wounding at least 18 people including four police officers. The blasts occurred near the hotel where Macron was reportedly staying, shortly after he departed for the presidential palace. Macron's office confirmed he was safe and that his meeting with Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa would continue as scheduled.

quiz.prompt: How many people were wounded in the Damascus explosions during Macron's visit?
quiz.explanation: Syria's Interior Ministry confirmed that at least 18 people, including four police officers, were wounded in the blasts.

cards.headline: French court clears Le Pen path to 2027 presidential race
cards.summary: A French appeals court has ruled Marine Le Pen guilty of misusing public funds but reduced the ban on her holding public office, clearing the way for her to run in the 2027 presidential election. However, the ruling comes with an electronic ankle tag condition. The decision is a significant development for the French far right ahead of the next election cycle.

quiz.prompt: What condition accompanies the French court's ruling allowing Le Pen to potentially run for president?
quiz.explanation: The Paris appeals court ruling clearing Le Pen's path to the presidential race comes with an electronic ankle tag condition.

cards.headline: Host USA knocked out of World Cup by Belgium
cards.summary: Belgium thrashed the United States 4–1 to eliminate the host nation from the FIFA World Cup 2026, leaving home fans stunned. The result sparked controversy after FIFA allowed US striker Folarin Balogun to play against Belgium following an eligibility reversal, which Belgium's Nicolas Raskin said fired his team up. US coach Mauricio Pochettino expressed disappointment over what he called the injection of "politics and manipulation" into the tournament.

quiz.prompt: What was the final score when Belgium eliminated the USA from the World Cup?
quiz.explanation: Belgium thrashed the USA 4–1 to knock the host nation out of the World Cup, according to multiple sources.

cards.headline: China test-launches ballistic missile in South Pacific
cards.summary: China conducted a ballistic missile test using a dummy warhead in the South Pacific, raising regional concerns. The United States called the test a "great concern" for the world, accusing China of nuclear proliferation. Notably, the launch took place on the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defence treaty specifically aimed at countering Chinese influence in the Pacific.

quiz.prompt: What notable event happened on the same day as China's South Pacific missile test?
options: The NATO summit opened in Ankara
Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defence treaty
The US imposed new sanctions on China
Japan launched its own missile test in response
quiz.explanation: The missile launch took place the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defence treaty meant to counter Chinese influence in the Pacific.

cards.headline: Suspect in Monaco bombing found dead in Ukraine
cards.summary: A Ukrainian woman suspected of carrying out a bomb attack targeting a Ukrainian millionaire in Monaco has been found dead with gunshot wounds in Ukraine. Police had been hunting Anastasiia Berezovska after the tycoon with links to Russia was injured in the blast. Reports suggest the killing was apparently carried out by a Ukrainian intelligence operative.

quiz.prompt: What happened to the woman suspected of carrying out the Monaco bomb attack?
options: She was arrested by Ukrainian police
She was found dead with gunshot wounds in Ukraine
She fled to a third country
quiz.explanation: The suspect, Anastasiia Berezovska, was found dead with gunshot wounds in Ukraine in what reports suggest was a killing carried out by a Ukrainian spy.

cards.headline: Supreme Court ruling deals blow to 1.3 million immigrants
cards.summary: The US Supreme Court issued a major ruling against roughly 1.3 million immigrants with Temporary Protected Status, many of whom have lived legally in the United States for decades. The decision was described as a huge blow for long-term TPS residents. Separately, the court also ruled that states may restrict participation in girls' and women's sports, in a decision affecting transgender athletes.

quiz.prompt: Approximately how many immigrants were affected by the Supreme Court's TPS ruling?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported that the Supreme Court ruling dealt a huge blow to roughly 1.3 million immigrants with Temporary Protected Status.

cards.headline: Half of Americans struggle to afford groceries and gas
cards.summary: An exclusive poll conducted for the Guardian found that roughly half of Americans are struggling to afford groceries and gas. About 57% of those polled believe the economy is worsening, painting a grim picture of the cost of living crisis. Additionally, 95% of Americans believe the US is facing significant economic challenges, according to the Harris survey.

quiz.prompt: According to the Guardian poll, what share of Americans believe the economy is worsening?
quiz.explanation: The Harris survey conducted for the Guardian found that about 57% of polled Americans believe the economy is worsening.

cards.headline: New clinical trials offer hope for deadly DRC Ebola strain
cards.summary: The Ebola strain currently ravaging the Democratic Republic of Congo has no specific approved treatments or preventive measures, leaving health workers without standard tools to combat it. Three new clinical trials are now underway that could provide possible breakthroughs for this particular strain. Researchers hope the trials will offer a pathway to treatments for what has been an especially difficult outbreak to address.

quiz.prompt: Why is the current Ebola outbreak in the DRC especially difficult to treat?
options: Health workers have refused to enter affected areas
The strain has no specific approved treatments or preventive measures
Funding for the response has been entirely cut off
The virus has mutated to become airborne
quiz.explanation: According to NPR, the strain responsible for the current DRC outbreak has no specific treatments or preventive measures, unlike other Ebola strains.

cards.headline: Maine Senate candidate faces calls to quit over assault allegation
cards.summary: Democratic US Senate candidate Graham Platner is facing growing pressure to drop out of the Maine race after a woman accused him of sexual assault, as reported by Politico. Several top Democratic figures have called for Platner to withdraw his candidacy, while Platner has denied the allegation. The controversy has dominated news cycles alongside the opening of the NATO summit.

quiz.prompt: What has Graham Platner done in response to the sexual assault allegation?
options: Suspended his campaign pending an investigation
Apologised and withdrawn from the race
quiz.explanation: According to multiple sources, Platner has denied the sexual assault allegation while facing growing pressure from Democratic figures to end his campaign.
